title: Effect of a single session of yoga and meditation on stress reactivity: A systematic review
journal: Stress and health : journal of the International Society for the Investigation of Stress
headline: Effect of a single session of yoga and meditation on stress reactivity: A systematic review
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of yoga examined brief & app-based practice. This systematic review synthesises the evidence for the effectiveness of a single session of yoga or its components including meditation and breathing techniques in reducing acute stress reactivity in healthy adults.
keyFindings: This systematic review synthesises the evidence for the effectiveness of a single session of yoga or its components including meditation and breathing techniques in reducing acute stress reactivity in healthy adults.
limitations: Risk of bias (ROB) was assessed using the Cochrane ROB 2 tool.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Effect of a single session of yoga and meditation on stress reactivity: A systematic review
journalName: Stress and health : journal of the International Society for the Investigation of Stress
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Stress and health : journal of the International Society for the Investigation of Stress (2024). This systematic review synthesises the evidence for the effectiveness of a single session of yoga or its components including meditation and breathing techniques in reducing acute stress reactivity in healthy adults.
whyItMatters: MoM shares breath regulation in a brief, guided format with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
